topic: Aspects of French-speaking Society: Current Trends
subject: french

items.q: How do you say 'I study maths' in French?
items.why: Students drop the article like English. School subjects in French take the DEFINITE article: 'j'aime le français', 'j'étudie les sciences', 'je déteste l'histoire'. This is the rule for general nouns.

items.correct: A verb in the subjunctive mood.
items.why: Students use infinitive after impersonal expressions. 'Il faut que tu PARTES' (subjunctive), not 'il faut que tu partir'. The que triggers subjunctive in the following clause.

items.q: Which verbs take 'être' as their auxiliary in the passé composé?
items.correct: Movement/state-change verbs (DR & MRS VAN DER TRAMP) and all reflexive verbs.
items.why: Students forget reflexives. Both groups use être: aller/venir/monter, etc. AND every reflexive verb (je me suis lavé). With être, the past participle agrees with the subject.

items.q: What is the correct future tense of 'changer' for 'il'?
items.why: Correct answer: changera. “changerait” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many députés sit in the Assemblée nationale?
items.why: Correct answer: 577. “348” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the Fifth Republic established?
items.why: The real figure is 1958. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
